name: TEXAS CAPITAL BANCSHARES INC/TX
business: Texas Capital Bancshares, Inc. is a registered bank holding company and full-service financial services firm that delivers customized solutions to businesses, entrepreneurs and individual customers. Its business activities are conducted primarily through its wholly-owned bank subsidiary Texas Capital Bank and its wholly-owned non-bank subsidiary TCBI Securities Inc., doing business as Texas Capital Securities. The company offers a variety of loan, deposit account, treasury management, investment banking, and private banking products and services.
sellsTo: Regional and national clients through mortgage finance, homebuilder finance, corporate and investment banking, and Bask Bank

exposures.sentence: risks related to the development and use of artificial intelligence
the Company's ability to effectively manage information technology systems, including third party vendors, cyber or data privacy incidents or other failures, disruptions or security breaches

TCBI also competes with other providers of financial services, such as non-bank financial institutions, commercial finance and leasing companies, consumer finance companies, financial technology companies, securities firms, insurance companies, full-service brokerage firms and discount brokerage firms, credit unions and savings and loan associations.

catalysts.event: Bank became a member of the Federal Reserve System
catalysts.sentence: Effective September 19, 2025, the Bank became a member of the Federal Reserve System.

catalysts.event: Share repurchase program authorized for up to $200 million
catalysts.sentence: On January 22, 2025, the Company's board of directors authorized a share repurchase program under which the Company may repurchase up to $200.0 million in shares of its outstanding common stock, excluding the effect of excise tax expense incurred on net stock repurchases.

catalysts.event: New share repurchase program authorized for up to $200 million
catalysts.sentence: Effective December 12, 2025, the Company's board of directors authorized a new share repurchase program under which the Company may repurchase up to $200.0 million in shares of its outstanding common stock, excluding the effect of excise tax expense incurred on the net stock repurchases.
The share repurchase program will expire on December 31, 2026, but may be suspended or discontinued at any time.

catalysts.event: Unsecured revolving line of credit maturity
catalysts.sentence: Unsecured revolving, non-amortizing line of credit with maturity date of February 8, 2027.
